content.problemStatement:
Create a C++ program that models different types of employees using dynamic method dispatch. \
You should have a base class `Employee` and one derived class called `Worker`.

The `Employee` base class should have the following attributes and methods:
- `name` (a string)
- virtual `void displayInfo()` - This method should be declared as virtual in the base class and should print the name of the employee.

The `Worker` class should inherit from `Employee` and have :
- `hourlyRate` (an integer)
- `displayInfo()` - method on each worker to display their information.

### Task
Your program should take the name and hourly rate of a worker as input and then create instances of Worker with this input. \
Use dynamic method dispatch to call the `displayInfo()` method on each worker to display their information by creating pointer to `employee` class.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- First line contain a string representing name of worker.
- Second line contain an integer representing rate of worker.
content.outputFormat: Use dynamic method dispatch to call the displayInfo() method on each worker to display their information.
